req.title: Card PIN change (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement enforces a rate limit of at most 5 PIN changes per 24-hour window for a given card. Key test partitions are: (1) a valid single PIN change within the window (EP positive), (2) exactly the 5th change in the window (BVA upper boundary - still allowed, kind=boundary), (3) the 6th change in the window (boundary+1 - must be rejected with 429, kind=boundary per BVA since it is the first value outside the valid range), (4) a PIN change on a card not belonging to the caller (NEG authorization, kind=negative), and (5) a state transition from under-limit to at-limit to over-limit (STC). The violation was that test[2] had kind=negative but heuristic=BVA; BVA boundary+1 tests are classified as kind=boundary because they probe the boundary of the input domain, not a general negative equivalence class. Fixed by changing kind to boundary for that test.

tests.name: test_single_pin_change_succeeds
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with valid token
Card belongs to the authenticated customer
No PIN changes have been made in the current 24-hour window for this card
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/pin with a valid new PIN payload
expected: Response body (if any) confirms PIN updated
trace: R-FIN-047: PIN change allowed within limit
EP: representative value from the valid-change partition (1 of 5)

tests.name: test_fifth_pin_change_is_allowed
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with valid token
Card belongs to the authenticated customer
Exactly 4 PIN changes have already been made within the current 24-hour window
expected: Rate-limit counter is now at its maximum (5)
trace: R-FIN-047: at most 5 changes per 24 h
BVA: exact upper boundary value (N = 5) must still succeed

tests.name: test_sixth_pin_change_returns_429
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with valid token
Card belongs to the authenticated customer
Exactly 5 PIN changes have already been made within the current 24-hour window
expected: Response body contains rate-limit error message or Retry-After header
trace: R-FIN-047: error pathway - 429 when rate limit exceeded
BVA: upper boundary + 1 (N = 6) must be rejected; first value outside the valid partition

tests.name: test_pin_change_on_unowned_card_is_forbidden
preconditions: Two distinct authenticated customers exist: owner and attacker
Card belongs to owner, not to attacker
No PIN changes made in the current window for this card
trace: R-FIN-047: precondition - card belongs to caller
NEG: unauthorized access attempt on another customer's card

tests.name: test_state_transition_under_limit_to_at_limit_to_over_limit
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with valid token
Card belongs to the authenticated customer
Rate-limit counter is reset (0 changes in current 24-hour window)
actions: Perform PIN changes 1 through 5 and assert each returns 200/204 (under-limit -> at-limit states)
Perform the 6th PIN change and assert it returns 429 (at-limit -> over-limit transition)
expected: System transitions correctly through: idle -> [1..4 changes] -> at_limit -> blocked
trace: R-FIN-047: full state lifecycle of rate-limit enforcement
STC: covers idle, incrementing, at-boundary, and exceeded states

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```json
{
  "requirement_id": "R-FIN-047",
  "reasoning": "The requirement enforces a rate limit of at most 5 PIN changes per 24-hour window for a given card. Key test partitions are: (1) a valid single PIN change within the window (EP positive), (2) exactly the 5th change in the window (BVA upper boundary - still allowed), (3) the 6th change in the window (BVA upper boundary + 1 - must be rejected with 429), (4) a PIN change on a card not belonging to the caller (NEG authorization), and (5) a state transition from under-limit to at-limit to over-limit (STC). Each test resets rate-limit state via a fixture that issues a unique card or resets the counter through an admin endpoint, ensuring idempotency. Internal double-quotes in JSON payloads are escaped with backslash.",
  "tests": [
    {
      "name": "test_single_pin_change_succeeds",
      "kind": "positive",
      "heuristic": "EP",
      "preconditions": [
        "Caller is authenticated with valid token",
        "Card belongs to the authenticated customer",
        "No PIN changes have been made in the current 24-hour window for this card"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "PATCH /cards/{id}/pin with a valid new PIN payload"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "HTTP 200 or 204 response",
        "Response body (if any) confirms PIN updated"
      ],
      "trace": [
        "R-FIN-047: PIN change allowed within limit",
        "EP: representative value from the valid-change partition (1 of 5)"
      ],
      "executable": "def test_single_pin_change_succeeds(fresh_card, auth_headers):\n    card_id = fresh_card[\"id\"]\n    payload = {\"pin\": \"1234\"}\n    resp = <api_client>.patch(\n        f\"/cards/{card_id}/pin\",\n        json=payload,\n        headers=auth_headers,\n    )\n    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), (\n        f\"Expected 200/204, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}\"\n    )\n"
    },
    {
      "name": "test_fifth_pin_change_is_allowed",
      "kind": "boundary",
      "heuristic": "BVA",
      "preconditions": [
        "Caller is authenticated with valid token",
        "Card belongs to the authenticated customer",
        "Exactly 4 PIN changes have already been made within the current 24-hour window"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "Exhaust 4 PIN changes via helper",
        "PATCH /cards/{id}/pin for the 5th time"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "HTTP 200 or 204 on the 5th request",
        "Rate-limit counter is now at its maximum (5)"
      ],
      "trace": [
        "R-FIN-047: at most 5 changes per 24 h",
        "BVA: exact upper boundary value (N = 5) must still succeed"
      ],
      "executable": "def test_fifth_pin_change_is_allowed(fresh_card, auth_headers):\n    card_id = fresh_card[\"id\"]\n    pins = [\"1111\", \"2222\", \"3333\", \"4444\"]\n    for pin in pins:\n        r = <api_client>.patch(\n            f\"/cards/{card_id}/pin\",\n            json={\"pin\": pin},\n            headers=auth_headers,\n        )\n        assert r.status_code in (200, 204), (\n            f\"Pre-condition setup failed on change {pin}: {r.status_code}\"\n        )\n    resp = <api_client>.patch(\n        f\"/cards/{card_id}/pin\",\n        json={\"pin\": \"5555\"},\n        headers=auth_headers,\n    )\n    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), (\n        f\"5th change should succeed; got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}\"\n    )\n"
    },
    {
      "name": "test_sixth_pin_change_returns_429",
      "kind": "negative",
      "heuristic": "BVA",
      "preconditions": [
        "Caller is authenticated with valid token",
        "Card belongs to the authenticated customer",
        "Exactly 5 PIN changes have already been made within the current 24-hour window"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "Exhaust 5 PIN changes via helper",
        "PATCH /cards/{id}/pin for the 6th time"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "HTTP 429 Too Many Requests",
        "Response body contains rate-limit error message or Retry-After header"
